talks.title: Better Than Bill? Teaching Hands-On Science
talks.speaker: Scott Sieke

questions.question: Bill Nye "the Science Guy" holds a degree in which field?
options: Physics
Biology
Mechanical Engineering
Chemistry

questions.question: Research on experiential learning suggests that hands-on activities improve retention compared to passive lecture. What cognitive mechanism primarily explains this?
options: Physical activity increases blood flow to the brain
Making and correcting errors generates stronger memory traces than passive observation
Hands-on tasks are always more enjoyable, reducing cortisol
Group work during labs socializes knowledge into long-term memory

questions.question: What is "pedagogical content knowledge" (PCK), as coined by Lee Shulman?
options: A teacher's total knowledge of their subject area
A teacher's understanding of how students commonly misunderstand specific content — and how to address those misconceptions
A standardized curriculum that all science teachers must follow
The set of classroom management skills that enable effective instruction

talks.title: Intertwined: Fiber Arts and the Labor Movement
talks.speaker: Evelyn Maguire

questions.question: The Luddites of early 19th-century England were workers in which industry who smashed machines to protest mechanization?
options: Coal mining
Textile manufacturing
Railroad construction
Steel production

questions.question: The Jacquard loom (1804) is considered a conceptual ancestor of modern computing. Why?
options: It used steam-powered pneumatic valves to automate weaving
It used punched cards to control weave patterns — a direct precursor to computer punch cards
It used electromagnetic relays to coordinate shuttle timing
It used clockwork gear sequences to automate the entire weaving cycle

questions.question: The Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire of 1911 killed 146 garment workers. What was the leading cause of the high death toll?
options: Inadequate fire suppression equipment in the building
Locked stairwell doors and insufficient fire escapes — workers could not escape
Workers wore flammable synthetic uniforms mandated by the factory
The building had no fire alarm system

originStory.text:
Hi. I'm Justin Fritz — and I'm a Nerd Nite Boss.

That's a real title. The Nerd Nite organization gave it to me, and I take it very seriously.

Nerd Nite started in Boston in 2003. Graduate student Chris Balakrishnan had been telling the same stories at his local bar — The Midway Cafe — about studying parasitic birds in Cameroon. The bartenders finally told him: just give a talk. One night. Slides. Get it out of your system.

He did. It worked.

In 2006, his roommate Matt Wasowski moved the concept to New York City, expanded it beyond science to any topic worth a drink and 20 minutes, and built it into a global brand — the "Discovery Channel with beer" — now in over 100 cities worldwide.

It came to Fort Collins because of me and one other very important person: Senne Van Loon.

But first — trivia.

Senne and I built this thing from scratch.

Senne is the kind of person who hears "let's put on a nerdy event at a bar" and says yes immediately — full commitment, no hesitation. That energy is rare and it matters. You cannot build something like this alone.

As we grew, Jamie Fritz joined us. Jamie is the backbone — always behind the scenes, making everything work, never needing the spotlight. The kind of person every organization needs and rarely thanks enough.

Jamie has been onstage exactly once: to introduce Marybeth Arcodia. Which brings me to Marybeth.

Marybeth Arcodia was our host.

She was brilliant at it — warm, sharp, funny in exactly the right way for a room full of nerds on their third drink. She held the stage like she was born for it.

Marybeth eventually left Fort Collins for a faculty position in Miami. We were sad to lose her and enormously proud of her.

Hannah Bauer stepped up as host after Marybeth — and she's been incredible.

Here's the thing you need to understand about Nerd Nite: once you're a Boss, you're always a Boss. Marybeth is still a Boss. Distance doesn't change that.

You just played Nerdometer.

Every edition is built around a real Nerd Nite Fort Collins event — the actual talks, the actual speakers, the real nerdy obsessions people bring to a bar and share with strangers.

Senne. Jamie. Marybeth. Hannah. Every speaker who ever trusted a room full of people with the thing they love most.

All Bosses. Always.

Come be part of it.

— Justin Fritz, Nerd Nite Fort Collins Boss
